title: What I want to leave my children's children
subtitle: A journal prompt set for examining what legacy and generational wealth mean to you specifically.
excerpt: A journal prompt set from Week 6 of the MONEY program. Five prompts for getting specific about what 'building wealth that lasts' means beyond the number — the values, habits, and opportunities you want to pass forward.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Week 6 daily reflections in The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom and Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use when generational wealth feels abstract or when you want to connect the financial building to what it is actually for.

content.text: A journal prompt set from Week 6 of the MONEY program. The prompts move from examining what was passed to you, to getting specific about what you want to pass forward.
New to journalling as a practice? Read
What did the generation before me leave — financially and in terms of money beliefs?
Write what was passed to you: practical inheritance, patterns, beliefs, silence. Note what served you and what didn't.
What pattern am I most determined not to pass forward?
Write the specific belief or behaviour. Name it plainly.
What do I want my grandchildren to inherit beyond money?
Write the values, habits, and relationship with money you want to pass forward. Be specific.
If I imagine the account I am building, what would I want its purpose to be for people I will never meet?
Write the purpose: education, choice, safety, opportunity. Give it a name if one comes.
What would I need to do in the next twelve months to start the generational wealth pattern I want to create?
Write two or three specific steps. Make them concrete enough to calendar.
Adapted from Week 6 daily reflections in The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom and Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
